name: Maria Bonita Restaurant
tagline: 28-year Chinatown Mexican institution — BYOB, no AC, just fans and the Mexican flag on the walls. The wet chile verde burrito is legendary.
mustOrder: wet chile verde burrito — smothered in green sauce, the signature
ahi burrito — Hawaiian-Mexican fusion done right
parking: Street parking on Hotel St; paid lots on Maunakea St
talkStory: Owner Diego started with a lunch wagon in Kailua/Waimanalo in 1998, named after his mother. Moved to this Chinatown spot and never left. No air conditioning, just fans. BYOB — bring your own beer or wine. The portions are crazy generous. The no-frills environment self-selects for people who care about food, not ambiance. Nearly three decades of authentic Mexican food in a hole-in-the-wall — the definition of a local gem. Lunch and dinner, closed Sun–Mon.
